title: Researcher PQC Workshop — All Industries, All Regions
whatToExpect: Cutting-edge PQC research surfaces — algorithm internals, security levels, side-channel, QKD
Modules for the standards landscape, hybrid combiners, SLH-DSA, stateful signatures, QKD
Page tours for /algorithms (full candidate set), /library (RFC corpus), /threats (research models)

steps.title: What you will leave with
steps.whyItMatters: A research-grade map of the PQC landscape and the open problems worth pursuing.
steps.expectedOutput: A research reading list and current open-problem inventory.
steps.narration: This workshop is the research-grade tour — algorithm candidates beyond the NIST primary picks, security levels math, side-channel testing, the IETF / standards process, hash-based and stateful signatures as long-life-key alternatives, and QKD as a parallel research thread.

cues.text: Researcher workshop — algorithm landscape, security models, side-channel, standards process.

steps.title: Confirm researcher persona
steps.whyItMatters: The persona drives research-relevant deep links and module visibility.
steps.narration: Researcher persona surfaces every advanced module and the full standards corpus.

cues.text: Persona definition — four picks shape every page. Researcher drives this flow.
Opening the persona wizard.
Step 1 — Track. Technical track unlocks developer, architect, ops, and researcher roles.
Step 2 — Role. Researcher surfaces the modules and tools tuned to your job.
Step 3 — Region. Filters timeline, leaders, threats and compliance to your jurisdiction.
Step 4 — Industry. Surfaces industry-specific threats, frameworks and vendors.
These four facets shape every workshop step. Change any one to re-personalize.

steps.title: Research Quantum Impact
steps.whyItMatters: The research-grade view — open problems, candidate algorithms beyond NIST, security model evolution.

steps.title: SLH-DSA
steps.whyItMatters: Hash-based signatures — stateless, conservative security, the long-key-lifetime fallback.
steps.narration: SLH-DSA (formerly SPHINCS+) — the stateless hash-based signature scheme. Conservative security assumptions, large signatures, the role as a long-key-lifetime fallback when ML-DSA is questioned.

cues.text: SLH-DSA — Hash-based signatures — stateless, conservative security, the long-key-lifetime fallback.

tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: Familiarity with /algorithms — full NIST candidate landscape

cues.text: NIST PQC algorithms — primary picks plus second-round candidates and withdrawn entries.
Security level math — NIST levels 1, 3, 5 vs concrete bit-security claims.

steps.title: /library — full standards and RFC corpus
steps.whyItMatters: The /library is the primary-source corpus — RFCs, drafts, FIPS docs, NIST publications.
tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: Familiarity with /library — full standards and RFC corpus
steps.narration: The /library is where every claim in the platform traces back to a primary source. RFCs, IETF drafts, FIPS 203/204/205, NIST IR series.

cues.text: /library — the full standards and RFC corpus. Every PQC claim traces to a primary source.
FIPS 203 standardises M L K E M. FIPS 204 standardises M L D S A. FIPS 205 standardises S L H D S A. Three canonical NIST standards.
IETF drafts on hybrid TLS, composite signatures, KEM combiners — track the active negotiations.

steps.title: /threats — research-relevant threat models
steps.whyItMatters: HNDL, HNFL, side-channel — the threat models that motivate research priorities.
tasks: Note the key affordances called out by the workshop
steps.narration: The /threats page enumerates the threats driving PQC research — HNDL (harvest-now-decrypt-later), HNFL (forge-later), side-channel families.

cues.text: /threats — the threat models that motivate PQC research and standards.
HNDL: encrypted traffic captured today, decrypted post-CRQC. The first-mover threat.
HNFL: signed artifacts harvested, forged post-CRQC. Long-lifetime signatures most affected.

steps.title: Quiz — test what you learned
steps.whyItMatters: A 5-to-45-minute quiz with persona-tuned topics gives you a quick measure of what stuck.
tasks: Note the duration slider and difficulty filter
See how persona-tuned topics are pre-selected
Run the quiz yourself after the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: A working knowledge of the quiz interface; you'll come back to actually take it.
steps.narration: The quiz is the quickest way to check what you've absorbed. Pick how much time you have, pick the topics that matter, and get a scored breakdown by category and difficulty.

cues.text: Test what you learned. Pick a duration, pick topics, go.
Duration slider. Five to forty-five minutes. Quiz scales the question count to match.
Difficulty filter. Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced. Multi-select.
Topic grid. Categories pre-filtered by your persona context.
Selected topics show a checkmark. The Start button activates once you pick at least one.
Start the quiz when you're ready. We'll skip running it inside the workshop.
Results show your score, category breakdown, difficulty breakdown, and let you retake or change topics.

steps.title: Recap and next research steps
steps.whyItMatters: The open-problem inventory you take into your next research cycle.
tasks: Pick one open problem to follow
steps.expectedOutput: A research reading list and an open-problem to track.
steps.narration: Three research takeaways: the standards process is your publication path, hybrid combiners and side-channel are active areas, and stateful signatures + QKD remain underexplored frontiers.

cues.text: Three research takeaways: standards process, hybrid + side-channel as live areas, stateful + QKD frontiers.
